name: Cabaletta Bio, Inc.
business: Cabaletta Bio is a late clinical-stage biotechnology company focused on developing engineered T cell therapies for autoimmune diseases, with lead product candidate rese-cel, a CD19-CAR T construct designed to reset the immune system.

exposures.sentence: We are a late clinical-stage biotechnology company focused on the discovery and development of innovative engineered T cell therapies that have the potential to provide deep and durable, perhaps curative, responses with one-time administration for patients with autoimmune diseases.

catalysts.event: Initiated enrollment in registrational DM/ASyS cohort
catalysts.sentence: We initiated enrollment in the registrational DM/ASyS cohort in December 2025 and anticipate BLA submission in 2027.

catalysts.event: Complete Phase 1/2 data from RESET-SLE cohorts anticipated
catalysts.sentence: Complete Phase 1/2 data from both cohorts is anticipated in the first half of 2026.

catalysts.event: Complete Phase 1/2 data from RESET-SSc cohorts anticipated
catalysts.sentence: Complete Phase 1/2 data from both cohorts is anticipated in the first half of 2026.

catalysts.event: Announce registrational cohort design for SSc
catalysts.sentence: We anticipate announcing the registrational cohort design for SSc in the first half of 2026.

catalysts.event: Complete Phase 1/2 data from RESET-MG cohorts anticipated
catalysts.sentence: Complete Phase 1/2 data from both cohorts is anticipated in the first half of 2026.

catalysts.event: Announce registrational cohort design for MG
catalysts.sentence: We anticipate announcing the registrational cohort design for studies in MG in mid-2026.

catalysts.event: First clinical experience from no-preconditioning and Cellares-manufactured rese-cel expected
catalysts.sentence: The first clinical experience from these patients is expected to be presented in the first half of 2026.

catalysts.event: Durability data from no-preconditioning and Cellares-manufactured rese-cel expected
catalysts.sentence: Durability data from the no-preconditioning SLE, LN and PV patients as well as the patients treated with rese-cel manufactured by the Cellares Cell Shuttle TM is expected to be presented in the second half of 2026.
We initiated enrollment in the registrational DM/ASyS cohort in December 2025 and anticipate BLA submission in 2027.

tamClaims.claim: Myositis affects approximately 80,000 patients in the U.S.
tamClaims.sentence: The three adult myositis subtypes, dermatomyositis, or DM, anti-synthetase syndrome, or ASyS, and immune-mediated necrotizing myopathy, or IMNM, being evaluated in the RESET-Myositis Phase 1/2 clinical trial of rese-cel affect approximately 80,000 patients in the U.S.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 20% to 25% of the prevalent myositis population, or 16,000 to 20,000 people, would be potentially eligible patients for rese-cel.
tamClaims.sentence: In the United States, we believe approximately 20% to 25% of the prevalent population, or 16,000 to 20,000 people, would be potentially eligible patients for rese-cel.

tamClaims.claim: SLE affects an estimated up to 320,000 patients in the U.S.
tamClaims.sentence: SLE affects an estimated up to 320,000 patients in the U.S., with LN as the most common end-organ manifestation, affecting approximately 30-40% of SLE patients.

tamClaims.claim: SSc affects approximately 90,000 patients in the U.S.
tamClaims.sentence: SSc affects approximately 90,000 patients in the U.S., typically middle-aged individuals, particularly women.

tamClaims.claim: gMG affects approximately 100,000 patients in the U.S.
tamClaims.sentence: gMG affects approximately 100,000 patients in the U.S.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 15,000 patients in the U.S. are affected by PV.
tamClaims.sentence: Approximately 15,000 patients in the U.S. are affected by PV.
